description: A designed Department of Labor slogan-and-news card promoting skilled trades. The upper portion is a darkened, warmly-lit photograph of a male tradesman/welder at work: he wears a dark coverall with hi-vis yellow reflective stripes and a soot-marked hard hat fitted with a headlamp, bending over equipment against a cinder-block wall with industrial machinery (a large circular pipe/flange and gears) around him. Overlaid across the lower-middle in a large mixed serif font is the slogan 'Learn a Trade, GET PAID!' ('Learn a Trade,' in italic, 'GET PAID!' in bold). Below sits a white rounded rectangle box topped by the black 'Forbes' logo, containing a verbatim excerpt in black serif: 'A college degree is no longer the only ticket to a high-paying job. A new LendingTree study finds that nearly 40% of Americans earning six-figure salaries never graduated from college. ...Traditional skilled trades continue to offer some of the most reliable paths to six-figure earnings without a degree.' The propaganda technique uses an aspirational dignity-of-labor image plus a reputable-outlet (Forbes) citation to promote blue-collar trades over four-year college, supporting the administration's pro-trades/apprenticeship workforce messaging. Target audience: young Americans, students, and prospective tradespeople weighing college vs. trades.
notable_text: Learn a Trade, GET PAID! Forbes: A college degree is no longer the only ticket to a high-paying job. A new LendingTree study finds that nearly 40% of Americans earning six-figure salaries never graduated from college. ...Traditional skilled trades continue to offer some of the most reliable paths to six-figure earnings without a degree.
